title: Inverter circuit arrangement
abstract: An inverter circuit arrangement that connects an IO-link master with a slave comprises an AB class transistor circuit (Qnc2, Qpc2) of which the currents are replicated by a current mirror (Qpo1, Qno1, Q po2 , Q no2 ) to a terminal of the slave (OUT1). A bias circuit (R b1 , Q b1 ,...., Q nc1 , Q pc1 ) provides bias voltages to the AB class transistors (Q nc2 , Q pc2 ) Â· A comparator (C) forms a feedback between the master and slave terminals (C/Q, OUT1). The circuit provides for a bidirectional inversion to make a slave device IO-link compatible.
claims:
Claims Claims : 1. An inverter circuit arrangement, comprising: a first terminal (OUT1), a second terminal (C/Q), a terminal for a first supply potential (V suppiy ) and a terminal for a second supply potential (V gnd ) ; a pull-up resistor (R 3 ) connected to the first terminal (OUT1) and to a terminal for the first supply potential (Vsupply) an AB class transistor circuit (Q nC 2, Q Pc 2) connected to the second terminal (C/Q) ; a first current mirror (Q poi , Q p0 2) having an input path connected to the terminal for the first supply potential (V suppiy ) and to one end of the AB class transistor circuit (Q nc 2 , Q PC 2 ) and having an output path connected to the first terminal (OUT1); a second current mirror (Q noi , Q nc u) having an input path connected to the terminal for the second supply potential (V gnd ) and to another end of the AB class transistor circuit (Q nC2 , Q PC 2 ) and having an output path connected to the first terminal (OUT1); a bias circuit (R bi , Q bi , Qnci, Q pci ) comprising a bias current source (R bi , Q bi ) , a terminal for a first bias voltage (V bn ) relative to the first potential (V suppiy ) and a terminal for a second bias voltage (V bp ) relative to the second supply potential (V gnd ) , wherein the terminals for the first and second bias voltages (V bn , V bp ) are connected to the AB class transistor circuit (Q nC2 , Q Pc 2) ; and a comparator (C) connected to the first terminal (OUT1) having an output connected to the second terminal (C/Q) through a third current mirror (Q d 2, Q di ) Â· 2. The inverter circuit arrangement according to claim 1, 

wherein the AB class transistor circuit comprises a PNP transistor (Q pC 2) and a NPN transistor (Q nC 2) , of which the emittors are connected to the second terminal (C/Q) . 3. The inverter circuit arrangement according to claim 1 or 2, wherein the comparator (C) comprises a first input (-) that is connected to a threshold voltage and a second input (+) connected to the first terminal (OUT1), wherein the output of the comparator is connected to a resistor (Ri) , wherein the third current mirror (Q d 2, Q di ) comprises an input path connected to the resistor and an output path connected to the second terminal (C/Q) . 4. The inverter circuit arrangement according to claim 3, wherein the first input (-) of the comparator (C) is an inverting input that is connected to a voltage divider (R 2 , R2) and the second input (+) of the comparator is a non inverting input connected to the first terminal (OUT1) . 5. The inverter circuit arrangement according to any of claims 1 to 4, a current mirror (Q pci , Q pC2 ) configured to receive the bias current (I b ) and having an output path that includes a transistor (Q pC2 ) of the AB class transistor circuit; another current mirror (Q nci , Q nc 2) configured to receive the bias current (I b ) and having an output path that includes another transistor (Q nC2 ) of the AB class transistor circuit. 6. The inverter circuit arrangement according to claim 5, wherein the current mirror (Q pci , Q pC 2) of the bias circuit comprises an input path that includes a diode (D 2 ) connected to the terminal for the first supply potential (V suppiy ) and a 

transistor (Q pci ) configured as a diode connected to the control terminal of the transistor (Q pC 2) of the AB class transistor circuit, wherein the other current mirror (Q nci , Qnci) of the bias circuit comprises an input path that includes a diode (Di) connected to the terminal for the second supply potential (Vgnd) and a transistor (Q nci ) configured as a diode connected to the control terminal of the other transistor (Q nC 2) of the AB class transistor circuit. 7. The inverter circuit arrangement according to any of claims 1 to 6, further comprising a master circuit (130) having an output terminal (131) connected to the second terminal (C/Q), the master circuit comprising a first switchable current source (134, IQL m ) connected to the output terminal (131) of the master circuit and the terminal for the second supply potential (V gnd ) , a second current source (133, ILL M ) connected to the output terminal (131) of the master circuit and the terminal for the second supply potential (V gnd ) and a third switchable current source (132, IQH M ) connected to the output terminal (131) of the master circuit and the terminal for the first supply potential (V suppiy ) . 8. The inverter circuit arrangement according to claim 7, further comprising a slave circuit (110) having an output terminal (111) connected to the first terminal (OUT1), the slave circuit comprising a switchable current source (112, IQL d ) connected to the terminal for the second supply potential (V gnd ) and to the output terminal (111) of the slave circuit . 9. The inverter circuit arrangement according to claim 8, 

wherein the terminal for the first supply potential is a terminal for a positive potential (V suppiy ) and the terminal for the second supply potential is a terminal for a ground potential (V gnd ) , wherein current mirrors connected to the terminal for the positive potential (V suppiy ) include PNP transistors and current mirrors connected to the terminal for the ground potential (V gnd ) include NPN transistors. 10. The inverter circuit arrangement according to claim 8 or 9, wherein the slave circuit (110) includes an open collector driver transistor of which the collector is connected to the first terminal (OUT1) and the emitter is connected to the terminal for the second supply potential (V gnd) . 11. The inverter circuit arrangement according to any of claims 8 to 10, wherein the master circuit (130) comprises an output stage that complies with European Norm EN 61131 part 9 and the slave circuit comprises an output stage that does not comply with European Norm EN 61131 part 9. 12. The inverter circuit arrangement according to any of claims 8 to 11, wherein the circuit is configured such that a current supplied by the output of the comparator (C) is higher than the current supplied by the second current source (133, ILL m) of the master circuit and smaller than the sum of currents supplied by the first switchable current source (134, IQL m) of the master circuit and the second current source (133, ILL M) of the master circuit.
